flags.concern: Over-engineering: Step 1 still introduces `useDropIndicator` only for the external drag path, while the repository keeps a second, separate drop-indicator implementation inside `useClipDrag`, so the plan adds an abstraction without removing the main duplication point.
flags.evidence: External drag indicator creation lives in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:155-204`. Internal clip-drag indicator creation is still separate in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:93-118`, including its own inline z-index/style writes. `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:49-51` explicitly describes internal clip drag and external HTML5 drag-drop as using the same indicator system, so leaving `useClipDrag` out of the extraction preserves the duplicated implementation.
